---
title: |
  Materials Genomics<br>Unit 11: Generative Models for Materials Discovery
bibliography: ref.bib
author:
  - name: Prof. Dr. Philipp Pelz
    affiliation:
      - FAU Erlangen-Nürnberg
execute:
  eval: false
format:
  revealjs:
    width: 1920
    height: 1080
    template-partials:
      - title-slide.html
    css: custom.css
    theme: custom.scss
    slide-number: c/t
    logo: "eclipse_logo_small.png"
    footer: "© Philipp Pelz - Materials Genomics"
---

## 01. Title: Generative Models for Materials Discovery
- Unit role and scope.

## 02. Learning outcomes
- Use generative models for candidate proposal.

## 03. Recap from Unit 10
- Multi-fidelity + physics-informed context.

## 04. Why generative design in materials
- Expand search beyond known compounds.

## 05. Representation choices
- Composition, crystal graph, latent vectors.

## 06. Validity constraints
- Chemistry and structure feasibility.

## 07. Generative model families overview
- VAE, GAN, diffusion, autoregressive.

## 08. VAE intuition
- Latent manifold learning.

## 09. Conditional VAE setup
- Goal-directed generation.

## 10. GAN intuition
- Generator-discriminator game.

## 11. Diffusion model intuition
- Iterative denoising generation.

## 12. Autoregressive generation
- Sequential structure composition.

## 13. Conditioning on target properties
- Goal-constrained candidate synthesis.

## 14. Latent space navigation
- Interpolation and optimization.

## 15. Validity vs novelty vs diversity
- Core generation tradeoff triad.

## 16. Metrics for generative quality
- Validity, uniqueness, novelty, utility.

## 17. Property predictor coupling
- Generator + evaluator loop.

## 18. Uncertainty in generated candidates
- Confidence-aware ranking.

## 19. Physics priors in generation
- Constraint-guided sampling.

## 20. Multi-fidelity evaluation loop
- Cheap screening to expensive validation.

## 21. Active-learning integration
- Iterative retraining with feedback.

## 22. Data scarcity and pretraining
- Transfer from broad corpora.

## 23. Bias in training databases
- Distributional blind spots.

## 24. Mode collapse risk
- Diversity failure patterns.

## 25. Hallucination and invalid structures
- Quality-control filters.

## 26. Synthetic accessibility constraints
- Lab feasibility checks.

## 27. Safety and sustainability constraints
- Responsible candidate filtering.

## 28. Human-in-the-loop curation
- Expert vetting of outputs.

## 29. Case sketch: alloy candidate generation
- End-to-end scaffold.

## 30. Case sketch: catalyst design
- End-to-end scaffold.

## 31. Case sketch: battery material proposal
- End-to-end scaffold.

## 32. Evaluation protocol
- Offline benchmark + wet-lab shortlist.

## 33. Reproducibility requirements
- Seed control and prompt/config logs.

## 34. Explainability for generated candidates
- Why was this candidate proposed?

## 35. Failure mode #1
- High novelty, low validity outputs.

## 36. Failure mode #2
- Predictor exploitation by generator.

## 37. Failure mode #3
- Low practical synthesizability.

## 38. Mitigation checklist
- Robust generation workflow.

## 39. Visualization patterns
- Latent maps and Pareto candidate plots.

## 40. Bridge to Unit 12
- Case studies and deployment pathway.

## 41. Exercise scaffold: train simple VAE
- Generate candidate set.

## 42. Exercise scaffold: apply validity filters
- Chemical plausibility checks.

## 43. Exercise scaffold: property-conditioned generation
- Targeted proposal run.

## 44. Exercise scaffold: diversity analysis
- Quantify coverage/novelty.

## 45. Exercise scaffold: shortlist report
- Candidate rationale with uncertainty.

## 46. Exam-oriented key statements
- High-yield concepts.

## 47. Summary table
- Model families vs tradeoffs.

## 48. Unit summary
- Core takeaways.

## 49. Reading assignment
- Materials discovery case studies.

## 50. References
- Source list.

::: {#refs}
:::
